objectives: Comprehensive architectural review of mx20022 workspace (7 crates)
Evaluate scalability, maintainability, adherence to architectural principles
Focus on codegen pipeline, validation architecture, translation layer, trait design
important_facts: Dependency graph is acyclic and well-layered: model(leaf) -> parse -> validate/translate -> cli
codegen is fully standalone (no internal crate dependency)
model crate has zero non-serde dependencies -- ideal data crate
Three-phase codegen pipeline: xsd->ir->emit with clean TypeGraph IR
Dual validation: Validatable trait (compile-time typed) + RuleRegistry (runtime extensible) + SchemeValidator (payment-scheme overlay)
Pattern codegen compiles XSD regex to inline byte checks at codegen time, avoiding regex dep in model
Translation helpers hardcoded to pacs_008_001_13 types (comment says layout identical to pacs.009/camt)
Feature flags on model only, not propagated through dependent crates
validate depends on model WITHOUT all features; parse/translate use features=[all] or specific families
Generated code is 17K+ lines per message version, uses newtype pattern with TryFrom validation
ChoiceWrapper workaround for quick-xml serde enum limitation is well-documented
FedNow validator has dual path: XML string scanning + typed downcast via Any
TranslationResult carries TranslationWarnings for data-loss transparency
28 generated message modules across 4 families (head, pacs, pain, camt)
risks_issues: MEDIUM: Feature flag asymmetry -- validate depends on model with default features only (pacs), meaning typed validation of non-pacs types fails unless downstream enables features
MEDIUM: Translation helpers hardcoded to pacs.008 concrete types, causing structural duplication across mapping modules
MEDIUM: SchemeValidator::validate_typed uses Any downcasting, losing compile-time type safety at the trait boundary
LOW: No unified error enum across workspace; each crate defines its own with #[from] composition
LOW: ConstraintError in model uses manual Display/Error impl while all other crates use thiserror
LOW: Decimal values stored as String (no semantic type) -- deferred per comment but creates validation-only-at-runtime risk
LOW: FedNow max_amount uses f64 despite integer-cent arithmetic elsewhere in same function

iteration.summary: Completed deep architectural review of all 7 crates in the mx20022 workspace. Read every Cargo.toml, lib.rs, and key internal files including the full codegen pipeline (xsd parser, IR types/lower, emit dispatcher, pattern codegen), model common types (validate, builder, choice), parse (de/ser/envelope), validate (rules registry, schemes, typed bridge, FedNow), and translate (MT parser/types, mapping modules, helpers). The architecture is well-structured with clean layering, proper separation of generated vs hand-written code, and thoughtful trait design. Key findings center on feature flag propagation gaps, translation helper coupling to specific message versions, and the Any-based scheme validation escape hatch.
inputs_consulted: Codegen: ir/types.rs, ir/lower.rs, emit/mod.rs, emit/validate.rs, emit/pattern_codegen.rs
Model: common/mod.rs, common/validate.rs, common/builder.rs, common/choice.rs, generated/mod.rs, generated/pacs/pacs_008_001_13.rs (partial)
Validate: lib.rs, error.rs, typed.rs, rules/mod.rs, schemes/mod.rs, schemes/fednow.rs
Translate: lib.rs, mt/mod.rs, mt/types.rs, mappings/mod.rs, mappings/error.rs, mappings/helpers.rs, mappings/mt103_to_pacs008.rs

mentions.relevance_note: Code quality findings complement architectural review
Test coverage gaps may indicate architectural boundary issues
Security findings overlap with validation layer design
